welcome_title: Welcome to the Implicit Association Test
welcome_p1: The IAT measures the strength of automatic associations between concepts — in this version, between flowers or insects and pleasant or unpleasant meanings.
welcome_p2: You will sort words into categories as quickly and accurately as possible by pressing the left (E) or right (I) key on your keyboard.
welcome_p3: The test takes about 7–10 minutes and is divided into 7 short blocks.
tutorial_step1_text: Two categories will be shown at the top-left and top-right of the screen. In combined blocks, two categories share the same side.
tutorial_step2_text: A word appears in the center. Decide which side it belongs to and press E for the left category or I for the right category.
tutorial_step3_text: If you press the wrong key, a red ✕ appears. Press the correct key to continue. Your first response is the one scored.
tutorial_step4_text: Respond as quickly as you can without making mistakes. The pairings change between blocks — pay attention to the category labels.
demo_intro: Here's what a trial looks like.
demo_step1: The category labels appear at the top corners — press E for left, I for right.
demo_step2: A word appears in the middle. 'rose' belongs to Flowers (left).
demo_step3: Correct response — the trial ends and the next word appears.
demo_step4: If you press the wrong key, a red ✕ shows. Press the correct key to continue.
demo_step5: You're ready. Click Start Practice to try it yourself.
practice_complete_text1: Good — you've seen all seven blocks. The real test uses the same structure but with more trials.
practice_complete_text2: Remember: respond as quickly as possible while staying accurate. The pairings reverse in the later blocks.
block_kind_target: Sort the words by type (flower vs. insect).
block_kind_attribute: Sort the words by meaning (pleasant vs. unpleasant).
block_kind_combined_compat: Sort by both — two categories share each side.
block_kind_combined_incompat: The pairings have changed — sort by both, but the sides are swapped.
block_intro_hint: Press Space or click the button to begin.
play_bottom_instruction: Press {{left}} for the left categories, {{right}} for the right categories.
results_dscore_hint: The D-score summarizes the reaction-time difference between compatible and incompatible pairings, normalized by variability. Positive values indicate faster responses when flower+pleasant and insect+unpleasant share a key.
dscore_interp_none: No reliable difference between the two pairings.
settings_trial_multiplier_desc: Scales the number of trials in each block (1.0× = standard IAT).
settings_iti_desc: Pause between trials after a correct response.
settings_font_size_desc: Size of the word shown in the center.
settings_error_penalty_desc: If enabled, the participant must press the correct key after an error before the trial advances.
footer_description: A reaction-time test that measures the strength of automatic mental associations between categories.
